div1.entryFree:
Constancy, 1) firmness of mind: All's II, 1, 87. H8 III, 2, 2 (?). Troil. I, 3, 21. Caes. II, 1, 227. Caes. II, 1, 227 II, 4, 6. Mcb. II, 2, 68.
2) faithfulness: there is written in your brow, provost, honesty and c. Meas. IV, 2, 163. whose --ies expire before their fashions, All's I, 2, 62. Especially in love: Sonn. 105, 7. 117, 14. 152, 10. Phoen. 22. Gentl. II, 2, 8. Tw. II, 4, 78. H5 V, 2, 161. Troil. III, 2, 168. Cymb. I, 4, 137.
3) consistency: all the story . . . grows to something of great c. Mids. V, 26. Perhaps also in H8 III, 2, 2: if you will now unite in your complaints, and force them with a c., the cardinal cannot stand under them (but cf. Ado II, 2, 55).
